titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Paul Raspe collection of Yale ensemble recordings
Paul Raspe collection of Yale ensemble recordings
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Anne Rhodes

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday September 26, 2024 at 00:01

did.unittitle: Paul Raspe Collection of Yale Ensemble Recordings

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright status for collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research. The finding aid includes links to stream many of the recordings online via Aviary. Yale users can login to Aviary using their Yale University ID, to stream these interviews without restriction. Other users are able to request access to interviews directly through Aviary.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: MSS 157, Paul Raspe collection of Yale ensemble recordings, Historical Sound Recordings, in the Music Library of Yale University.

did.physdesc: Program and assorted notes about performance / recording

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

did.unittitle: Dixit Dominus (George Friedrich Handel) and Mass in D minor (Franz Joseph Haydn)

odd.head: General
odd.p: Tracks 1-10: Dixit Dominus; tracks 11-22: Mass in D minor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With the New Haven Symphony Orchestra (Michael Palmer, music director)

accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Tracks 1-7: Mass no. 3 in F minor; tracks 11-14: Three motets

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With the New Haven Symphony (Michael Palmer, music director)

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

did.unittitle: Building community: a concert to benefit Habitat for Humanity of Greater New Haven
did.physdesc: Program and assorted notes about performance / recording

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With Garmon Ashby, principal assistant conductor and Douglas Dickson, accompanist.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With Richard Gard, guest conductor

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

did.physdesc: Program and assorted notes about performance / recording

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: 1. Johannes Ockeghem, "Alma redemptoris mater," motet, 2. Ockeghem,"Au travail suis," rondeau; Ockeghem, "Missa Au travail suis": 3. Kirie, 4. Gloria, 5. Credo, 6. Sanctus, 7. Agnus dei. Side B: 8. Robert Morton, "La Perontina," instrumental, 9. Ockeghem, "L'autre d'antan," rondeau, 10. Joan Abrosio Dalza, "Calata ala Spagnola," lute, 11. Antoine Busnoi, "A qui vens tu tes coquilles," voils, 12. Ockeghem, "Petitte camusette," rondeau, 13. Ockeghem "Ma bouche rit," bergerette, 14. Johannes Vincenet, "Fortune, par ta cruaulté," voils. 15. unknown, 16. unknown.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

did.unittitle: The Yale Camerata and The Yale Collegium Musicum in a program of Buxtehude's concerted vocal music

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: 1. "Hertzlich lieb hab ich dich, o Herr" (BuxWV 41), 2. "Laudate pueri" (BuxWV 69), 3. "Der Herr ist mit mir" (BuxWV 15), 4. "Ad cor, from the cantata cycle Membra Jesu" (BuxWV 75). Side B: 1. "Fürwahr, er trug unsere Krankheit" (BuxWV 31), 2. "Ich suchte des Nachts in meinem Bette" (BuxWV 50), 3. "Ihr lieben Christen, freut euch nun" (BuxWV 51).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Guillaume Dufay: 1-2. "Ave regina caelorum," three-voice motet, 3-5. Kyrie from the missa "Ave regina caelorum," 6. "Vergene bella," stropha, 7. "Flos florum, motet," 8. "Donnés l'assault," rondeau, 9. "La belle se siet,"ballade, 10. "J'atendray tant," rondeau, 11-12. Gloria from the missa "Ave regina caelorum." Arnold de Lantinis: 13. "In tua memoria Lauda." Johannes Ciconia: 14. "Con lagreme,"ballata," 15. "O rosa bella," ballata, 16. "Ligiadra donna," ballata, 17. "Una panthera," madrigale, 18. "O felix templum," motet, 18. "Gloria," mass fragment. Dufay: 20-12. "Ave regina caelorum," four-voice motet), 22-23. Agnus dei from the missa "Ave regina caelorum."

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side 1: 1. "Tota pulchra es," four-voice motet (Heinrich Issac), 2. "Ibo michi ad montem," three-voice motet, solo (Leonel Power), 3. "Quam pulchra es," three-voice motet (John Dunstable), 4. "Quam pulchra es," three-voice motet, tenor and voils (Power), 5. "Song of Solomon" cycle, six four-voice motets (Leonhard Lechner), 6. "Surgam et circuibo," five-voice motet (Giovanni Pierluigi da Palestrina), 7. "Fantasia," three voils (Thomas Lupo), 8. "Tota pulchra es," four-voice motet (Orlando di Lasso). Side 2: 1."Sonata seconda detta la Casalasca," two viols with basso continuo (Salomone Rossi), 2. "Ich beschwöre euch," dialogus; seven-voice motet with basso continuo (Heinrich Schütz), 3. "Nigra sum," concerto; tenor with basso continuo (Claudio Monteverdi), 4. "Sonata sopra L'aria di Ruggiero," two violins with basso continuo (Rossi), 5. "In lectulo per noctem," concerto; soprano, alto, and three viols with basso continuo (Schütz), 6. "Meine Schwester, meine Braut," six-voice motet (Melchoir Franck). 7. [applause]

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: 1. "Meine Seele verlanget nach deinem Heil" (Heinrich Schütz), 2. "De profundis clamavi," Psalm 129 (Heinrich Biber), 3. "Vater unser" (Schutz); "Mensa sonora" (Biber): 4. sonata, 5. aria, 6. canairo, 7. amener, 8. ciacona, 9. sonatina; 10. "Wie habe ich dein Gesetz so lieb" (Schutz). Side B: 1. "Dixit Dominus," Psalm 109 (Biber), 2. "Arch dass ich Wassers gnug hatte," lamento (J.C. Bach), "Mensa sonora" (Biber): 3. gagliarda, 4. sarabanda, 5. aria, 6. ciacona, 7. sonatina; 8. "Laudate Dominum," Psalm 116 (Biber), 9. "Jauchzet dem Herren, alle Welt" (Schutz), 10. [applause]

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

did.unittitle: Les Goûts Réünis: Italian, French, and German Baroque music

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: 1. "Unser Leben ist ein Schatten," double chorus (Johann Michael Bach), 2. Sonata in G minor (Georg Muffat), 3. "Wie der Hirsch schreiet nach frischem Wasser," motet (Christoph Bernhard), 4."Litaniae Lauretanae" (Heinrich Biber) Side B: 5. "Messe pour le Samedy du Pasques" (Marc-Antoine Charpentier), 6. "La Paix du Parnasse," trio sonata in G major (Francois Couperin), 7. "Jonas," oratorio for Lent (Giocomo Carissimi).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: 1. "In Gottes Namen fahren wir," motet (Anonymous), 2. "O rosa bella," song (John Bedingham), 3. "O rosa bella," intabultion (Anon.), 4. "In Feuers Hitz," tenorlied (Anon.), 5. "O rosa bella/In Feuers Hitz", quodlibet (Anon.), 6. "Gross Sehnen," song (Anon.); "Missa Gross Sehnen" (Anon.): 7. Kyrie, 8. Gloria, 9. Sanctus, 10. Agnus Dei. Side B: 11. Three instrumental pieces from the Glogauer Liederbuch: "Die Eselskrone," "Der Pfauenschwanz," "Die Katzenpfote" (Anon.), 12. "La Martinella" (Johannes Martini), 13. "Ein frlohlich Wesen," song (Jacobus Barbireau); "Missa ein frohlich Wesen" (Heinrich Isaac): 14. Kyrie, 15. Agnus Dei, 16. "Es wollt ein Meydlein grasen gan," song (Isaac); 17. "In meinem Sinn," instrumental piece (Isaac), 18. "Carmen," instrumental piece (Isaac), 19. "Innsbruck, ich muss dich lassenm" song (Isaac).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

did.unittitle: What is our life? A program of Elizabethan and Jacobean music

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: 1. "In jejunio et fletu," motet (Thomas Tallis), 2. "Fantasia" (William Byrd), 3. "Emendemus in melius," motet (Byrd), 4. "In nomine" (Tallis), 5. "Fantasia" (Robert White), 6. "Gaude gloriosa Dei mater," motet (Tallis). Side B: 1. "What is our life?" madrigal/motet (Orlando Gibbons), 2. "Dr. Case's Pavan" (John Dowland), 3. "The most sacred Queen elizabeth, her Galliard" (Dowland), 4. "Go, crystal tears," air (Dowland), 5. "Report upon 'When shall my sorrowful sighing slack'" (John Black), 6. "Fair is the Rose," madrigal/motet (Gibbons); "Trilogy from a Pilgrimes Solace" (Dowland): 7. "Thou mighty God," 8. "When David's life," 9. "When the poor cripple; 10. "This sweet and merry month of May," madrigal (Byrd), 11. "Canzonet" (Thomas Morley), 12. "O sweet woods" (Dowland), 13. "Fine knacks for ladies" (Dowland), 14. [applause]

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: 1. "Marian Antiphone: Salve Regina" (Paolo Quagliati), 2. "Psalm 112: Laudate pueri Dominum" (Claudio Monteverdi), 3. "Antiphon for Vespers on Christmas Day: Tecum principium chant" (Monteverdi), 4. "Psalm 109: Dixit Dominus" (Monteverdi), 5. "Sonata in C (Johann Rosenmuller), 6. "Psalm 111: Beatus Vir" (Giovanni Rosenmuller). Side B: 7. "Sopra la Monica," sonata (Briagio Marini), 8. "Deus tuoroum militum," (Monteverdi), 9. "Antiphon for Vespers on Christmas Day: Hodie Christus natus est," chant (Monteverdi), 10. "Magnificat" (Giovanni Rovetta), 11. "La Bevilaqua," sonata da chiesa a 3 (Giovanni Legrenzi), 12. "Marian antiphon: Ave Sanctissima Maria" (Quagliati), 13. "Gloria a 7" (Monteverdi).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

did.unittitle: An international repertory of the sixteenth century: Orlando di Lasso, 1532-1592

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: 1. "Tui sunt caeli," double-chorus motet, 2. Kyrie from Missa ad imitationem moduli "Susanne un jour," chanson, 3. "Bicinium," 5. From the motet cycle "Prophetiae Sibyllarum:" "Carmina chromatico," "Sibylla Delphica," "Sibylla Agrippa," 6. "Bicinium," 7. "Psalmus secundus poentitentials: Psalm 31: Beati quorum remissae sunt," psalm motet. Side B: 8. "Ardo, si ma non t'amo," madrigal,/ solo, 9. "Sotto quel sta," madrigal, 10. "Je l'ayme bien," chanson, 11. "La nuict froide et sombre," chanson, 12. "Tota pulchra es," motet (Emanuel Andrienssen), 13. "Madonna mia pieta," 14. "Quand mon mari," chanson, 15. "Un jour vis un foulon," chanson, 16. "Lucescit / Nunc bibamus," macaronic chansons, 17. "Three Branles from Adrian le Roy, Premier Livre," 18. "Baur', was trägst im Sacke," Lied, 19. "Ich weiß mir ein Maidlein hübsch und fein," Lied, 20. "Musica," motet.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: 1. "Meine Seele erhebet den Herren," chorale prelude (Johann Michael Bach), 2. "Lieber Herr Gott," motet (Johann Christoph Bach), 3. "Furchte dich nicht," motet (J. C. Bach), 4. "Ach, dass Wassers g'nug hatte," concerto (J. C. Bach), 5. "Warum betrübst du dich, mein Herz," chorale prelude (J. M. Bach), 6. "Ich lasse dich nicht," motet (J. S. Bach), 7. "Ach, wie sehnlich," song (J. M. Bach), 8. "Unser Leben währet siebsig Jahr," motet (J. M. Bach), 9. "Prelude and Fugue in E-flat" (J. C. Bach). Side B: 1. "Furchtet euch nicht," motet (J. M. Bach), 2. Missa "Angelus ad Pastrores": Credo (Hieronymous Praetorius). 3. "Sei, lieber Tag, willkommen," motet (J. M. Bach), 4. "Meine Frieundin, du bist schön," dramatic cantata (J. C. Bach.)

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

did.unittitle: Celebrating the four hundred and fiftieth birthday of William Byrd, 1543-1623

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: 1. "The Fairy-round," galliard (Anthony Holborne), 2. from "Psalms, songs and sonnets:" "Sing ye our Lord a new song," 3vv (William Byrd); from "Mass for 5 voices:" 3. Kyrie, 4. Gloria, 5. Agnus dei; 6. from "Ladye Nevell's Booke:" a voluntary (Byrde), 7. From "Cantiones Sacrae:" "Vide, Domine, afflictionem nostram," 5vv (Byrd), 8. from "Songs of Sundrie Natures:" "Come to me grief, for ever," elegy on the death of Sir Philip Signey (Byrd) 9. "Fantsasia" (Byrd), 10. from "Songs of sundrie natures:" "Ye sacred muses," elegy for Thomas Tellis (Byrd). 11. from "Psalms, songs, and sonnets:" "Come woeful Orpheus," 5vv (Holborne), 12. "Mens innovata," pavane (Holborne). Side B: 1. from "The Great Service:" Magnificat, 5-10vv (Byrd), 2. "Browning" (Elway Bevin); from "Two dances from the Fitzwilliam book:" (Byrd) 3."Wolsey's wilde," 4. "La volta" (Byrd); "Browning" (Byrd), 6. from "Psalmes, sonnets, and songs:" "I joy not in no eathly bliss" (Byrd), 7. "The honie-suckle dance," almaine (Holborne), 8. "The widowes myte," galliard (Holborne), 9. from "Songs of sundrie natures:" "Though Amarillis dance in green" (Byrde), 10. from "The Great Service:" "Nunc dimittis," 5-10vv (Byrd), 11. [special]

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

did.unittitle: Advent in the Renaissance: the feast of the conception of the Holy Virgin, as celebrated at the Cathedral of Cambrai, ca. 1520

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side A: 1. Processional antiphon: "Conceptio tua" (plainchant/falsobordone), 2. Introit: "Gaudeamus omnes" (plainchant); from "Missa gaudeamus" (Josquin des Prez): 3. "Kyrie eleison," 4-5. "Gloria in excelsis Deo;" 6. Collect: "Famulis tuis Domine" (intonation formula), Epistle (Isaiah 11:1-5) (intonation formula); 7. Gradual: "Propter veritatem" V. "Audi filia" (chant) 8. "Alleluia" V. "Conceptio gloriosae" (chant), sequence: "Mittit ad Virginem" (chant); 9. Gospel (Matthew 1:1-6) (intonation formula), 10. from "Missa gaudeamus" (Josquin): "Credo in unum Deum." Side B: 11. Offertory: "Felix namque es" (chant), 12. Secret (silent prayers), 13. Preface (intonation formula), 14-15. from "Missa Gaudeamus" (Josquin): "Sanctus," 16. "Pater noster" (formulaic chant), "Pax Domini" (formulaic chant), 17-18. from "Missa Gaudeamus" (Josquin): "Agnus Dei," 19. Communion: "Beata vescera" (chant), 20. Postcommunion: "Sumpsimus Domine" (intonation formula), 21. Motet: "Ave Maria" (Josquin).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

did.unittitle: Le faccie d'amore: Ariosto's "Orlando furioso" in music of the sixteenth century

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: SIDE A: Desire (Cipriano de Rore): 1. "Era il bel viso suo," 2. "E ne la face;" Infatuation (Marc'Antonio Ingegneri): 3. "Se'l sol si scosta;" Fidelity: 4. Soneto V, "Ruggier, qual sembre fui" (Enríquez de Valderrabano), 5. "Tastar de corde" (Joan Ambrosio Dalza), "Ruggieri" (Vincenzo Galillei); Sex (Vincenzo Ruffo): 6. "Non romor di tamburi;" Inconstancy (Hoste da Reggio): 7. "Guardatevi de questi," 8. "Cosí fan questi giovani," 9. "Non vi vieto;' Suspicion (Orlando di Lasso): 10. "Spesso in poveri alberghi;" Jealousy (Domenico Maria Ferrabosco): 11. "Lisdegni, le repulse;" Betrayal (Adrea Gabrieli): 12. "E dov non potea," 13. "Ma i venti che portavano," 14. "E con la faccia in giù." SIDE B: Misery (Bartolomeo Trombocino): 15. "Queste non son più lacrime;" Madness: 16. "Chi salirà per me" (Giches de Wert), 17. "Sonata sopra l'Aria di Ruggiero" (Salamone Rossi); The Folly of Love (Jaques Arcadelt): 18. "Ingiustissim' Amor."

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

did.unittitle: Christmas in Paris: a concert of choral music by Mouton, Sermisy, Charpentier, et al.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: SIDE A: 1. "Noe, noe, psallite noe" (Jean Mouton), 2. "Noe, noe, magnificatus est rex pacificus" (Claudin de Sermisy), 3. "Noel, sors de ton lit paré" (Eustache Du Caurroy); "Première Suitte de Pièces à deux Dessus", Op. 4 (Jacques Hotteterre): 4. Gravement, Gay, 5. Allemande, 6. Rodeau tendre, gracieusement, 7. Rondeau gay, 8. Gigue. SIDE B: "Messe de minuit pour Noël" (Marc-antoine Charpentier): 9. Kyrie, 10. Gloria, 11. Credo, 12. Offertory interlude, 13. Sanctus, 14. Agnus Dei.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

did.unittitle: Petrarca in musica: poems from the Canzoniere in music of the sixteenth century

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1. "Quante volte diss'io" (Adrian Willaert); 2. "Mia benigna fortuna," 3. "Crudele, acerba" (Cipriano de Rore); 4. "Mai non vo' più cantar" (Claudio Merulo), 5. "Proverbio 'Ama chi t'ama'" (Francesco Bonardo), 6. "In silenzio" (Leandro Mira), 7. "De' passati miei danni" (Danielle Grisonio), 8. "Amor, quando fioriva" (Giovanni Pierluigi da Palestrina), 9. "Hor vedi Amor" (Luca Marenzio); 10. "O cameretta", 11. "Né pur il mio secreto" (Giaches de Wert); 12. "Chiare fesche et dolci acque," 13. "S'egli è pur mio destino," 14. "Tempo verrà ancor forse," 15. "Da' be' rami scendea," 16. "Quante volte diss'io" (Jacques Arcadelt).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.

did.unittitle: Nunn komm der heide Heiland: music of the Lutheran Reformation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1. "Veni, redemptor gentium;" "Nun komm, der Heiden Heiland;" "Nun komm, der Heiden Heiland à 4" 2. "Nun komm, der Heiden Heiland à 4" (Machael Praetorius), 3. "Unsäglich Schmerz" (Ludwig Senfl), 4. "Nun komm, der Heiden Heiland à 4" (Balthasar Resinarius), 5. "Nun komm, der Heiden Heiland à 5" (Johann Walter), 6. "Diligam te Domine" (Josquin Desprez), 7. "Nun komm, der Heiden Heiland à 2 ('auf Muteten Art')" (Praetorius), 8. "Dulces exuviae" (Anon), 9. "Nun komm, der Heiden Heiland à 3 ('auf muteten Art')" (Praetorius), 10. "Anima mea liquefacta es" (Orlande de Lassus), 11. "Veni redemptor gentium à 5 ('ad imitationem Orlando de Lasso auf Muteten Art')" (Praetorius), 12. "Nun komm, der Heiden Heiland à 3 ('auf Madrigalischer Art')" (Praetorius), 13. "Nun komm, der Heiden Heiland à 3 ('auf Clausel Art')" (Praetorius), 14. "Egredimini et videte" (Andrea Gabrieli), 15. "Nun komm, der Heiden Heiland à 8" (Praetorius), 16. [applause], 17. "Ecce quan bonum" (Senfl), 18. "Nun komm, der Heiden Heiland à 4 ('Fugeweiß ad aequales')" (Praetorius), 19. "Potrò viver io più" (Luca Marezio), 20. "Veni redemptor gentium à 6 ('auf Ludovici de Victoria Art')" (Praetorius), 21. "Vidi speciosam sicut columbam" (Tomás Luis de Victoria), 22. "Veni redemptor gentium à 6 ('auf Ludovici de Victoria Art')" (Praetorius), 23. "Beati immaculati/Vivat Ioannes Friderich/Vive Luthere" (J. Walter).

accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original computer files may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use.
